name: Canada federal - CBCA
description: Federal business corporation guide tracks for CBCA annual maintenance, corporate records, securities registers, and ISC records.

rules.summary: CBCA annual workflows include AGM timing, annual financial disclosure to shareholders, and the annual return obligation.
rules.tooltipText: Use this for federal annual meeting and filing workflows: first AGM timing, later AGM timing, annual financial statements, and annual return reminders.
rules.caveatText: Corporations Canada policy says active CBCA corporations generally file annual returns within 60 days after the anniversary date; court extensions and Director forms can affect details.

rules.summary: CBCA corporations must maintain core corporate records, including articles, by-laws, minutes/resolutions, filed notices, securities records, and ISC records.
rules.tooltipText: Use this citation track for minute-book completeness, securities register, filed director/registered-office notices, and individuals-with-significant-control records.
rules.caveatText: Access rights, privacy treatment, and retention details vary by record type and requester.

rules.summary: Subject to the articles or by-laws, a majority of the directors, or the minimum number required by the articles, constitutes directors' meeting quorum.
rules.tooltipText: Use the articles and by-laws first; where they do not override, apply the CBCA directors' quorum default.
rules.caveatText: Vacancies and article/by-law provisions can affect whether remaining directors may act.

rules.summary: A CBCA special resolution generally requires at least two-thirds of the votes cast, or written signature by all shareholders entitled to vote.
rules.tooltipText: Use this for federal special-resolution threshold checks and unanimous written special resolutions.
rules.caveatText: Specific transactions, class votes, articles, by-laws, or unanimous shareholder agreements may add separate approval requirements.
